description: Forward-compatibility hash verification for DataRef openness. A future-version producer adds an unknown producer-controlled field ('future_field' here, illustrative) at the root of a DataRef inside body.data_refs[]. Because each DataRef is part of ProducerContent (the content_hash preimage, RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.7), the unknown field is covered by the producer signature. A v0.1.0 consumer that recomputes content_hash from the raw received JSON — preserving all unknown DataRef fields — produces the same hash the producer signed. This fixture pins the canonical form and SHA-256 so v0.1.0 consumers can verify their DataRef deserialization path is forward-compatible. A consumer that drops 'future_field' before hashing (typical typed-struct deserialization of data_refs[] without a catch-all) would compute a different hash and falsely raise hash_mismatch — see RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.7 'DataRef Schema Openness' and RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.7 'Hash verification over raw JSON'.

vectors.name: Body whose data_ref carries an unknown producer-controlled field 'future_field' — included in hash
vectors.consumer_behavior: REQUIRED. When a v0.1.0 consumer encounters this body shape, it MUST recompute content_hash over the raw parsed JSON (with the body-level exclusion set fields removed by name). The unknown 'future_field' inside data_refs[0] MUST appear in the canonical input. JCS sorts the DataRef object keys to future_field, location, type. The recomputed hash MUST equal the value above; only then does the consumer verify the signature.
vectors.non_conformant_behavior: A consumer that deserializes data_refs[] into a typed DataRef struct without an unknown-field catch-all will lose 'future_field' before hashing, compute sha256 over a 2-key DataRef object instead of the 3-key one above, and raise a false hash_mismatch. DataRef is an OPEN schema (additionalProperties: true) precisely to forbid this silent break — see RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.7.

notes: Pairs with can-008 (unknown field at the body root) and can-009 (exclusion-set membership is by name). can-010 is the DataRef-nested analogue: openness extends one level down into data_refs[]. The nested `embedded` sub-object is, by contrast, CLOSED — see schema-003.
